page: Vultr voc-g-24c-96gb-480s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 24 vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the server is powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 24 physical cores with a single thread per core configuration and utilizes a shared CPU allocation model. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware support for AVX, AES cryptography, and SHA-NI instructions.

Storage is bundled with 480 GB of local NVMe SSD, providing fast read and write capabilities directly attached to the instance. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong performance characteristics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance reaches 3208.88, placing it in the top tier (top 10%), while the multi-core CPU performance scores 74749.71, ranking in the top 25%. This configuration delivers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads, though the shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, isolated CPU performance.

This server is suitable for multi-threaded applications, general-purpose web serving, database hosting, and moderate batch processing tasks that benefit from high core counts and fast local NVMe storage without requiring dedicated CPU execution.
description: Vultr voc-g-24c-96gb-480s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 24 vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features a shared CPU allocation with 24 physical cores and a single thread per core. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 3208.88 (top 10%) and strong multi-core performance at 74749.71 (top 25%). The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a balanced profile, while the local NVMe storage supports high-throughput data access. This server offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense workloads, making it well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, and general-purpose web serving where dedicated CPU isolation is not strictly required.
